displayTitle: Born in the Mold: Ingot Flaws & Echoes

beats.narration: Before it was plate or pipe, your test piece was a giant casting, and its flaws were born in the first minutes of freezing.
An ingot freezes from the mold wall inward: chill skin, columnar grains, then a coarse center. The last liquid, top center, collects the trouble.

payload.heading: How an ingot freezes — outside in
payload.note: LAST TO FREEZE: TOP CENTER — TROUBLE LIVES HERE

beats.narration: That trapped liquid leaves three calling cards: shrinkage becomes centerline pipe, gas becomes blowholes and porosity, rejected solute becomes segregation.

payload.heading: What the last liquid leaves behind

beats.narration: Coarse grain buries five megahertz in grass, so drop to one or 2.25 megahertz. Pipe, struck broadside, rings loud; porosity murmurs; segregation barely whispers.

payload.heading: How ingot flaws answer the beam
payload.note: LOUD · MURMUR · WHISPER — READ THE SCREEN

beats.narration: So sketch the freeze before you scan: predict each flaw, aim broadside, and read a sinking back wall as a flaw speaking.

payload.sub: Know the freeze, know the flaw.
